vulnerability_detection.justification: All three critical vulnerabilities identified with accurate attack-vector descriptions: stored XSS via innerHTML in render() (server data rendered as HTML), reflected XSS in search() (raw query string into innerHTML), and JavaScript injection via the inline onclick handler. No exploitable vulnerability was left unmentioned.

fix_correctness.justification: Every fix addresses the root cause: render() uses createElement + textContent for all user-supplied fields, search() builds the result paragraph with textContent, and the delete button is wired via addEventListener passing the id as a JS value rather than a code string. No fix is bypassable or introduces a new vulnerability.

fix_completeness.justification: All three identified XSS/injection vulnerabilities have corresponding code fixes applied. The AUDIT.md documents each finding and its fix. A bonus fix for the addComment race condition was also applied.

regression_avoidance.justification: All original functionality is preserved: comments render with author (strong), date (.date span), body (.div.body), and a delete button; search filters and displays result count; loadComments and addComment API contracts are unchanged (addComment gaining async is a backwards-compatible improvement).

framework_idiom.justification: Core fixes are idiomatic modern vanilla JS (createElement, textContent, append, addEventListener). Two minor non-idiomatic patterns: container is cleared with innerHTML = '' instead of the preferred replaceChildren(), and a private #escapeHtml method is defined but used only for the element ID — redundant since property assignment (.id = ...) does not go through HTML parsing.

expectations_met: FIXES: Replaces innerHTML concatenation in render() with DOM APIs (createElement, textContent, appendChild)
FIXES: Replaces innerHTML in search() with DOM APIs / textContent
FIXES: Replaces inline onclick handler with addEventListener
PRESERVES: All comment fields (author, date, body) still render
PRESERVES: Delete button still works with addEventListener
PRESERVES: Search still filters and shows result count
PRESERVES: Class structure and API interface remain the same
DOES NOT: Suggest DOMPurify as the primary fix — DOM APIs used throughout
DOES NOT: Flag the fetch GET request as a vulnerability
AUDIT: Identifies render() innerHTML as stored XSS (server data rendered as HTML)
AUDIT: Identifies search() innerHTML as reflected XSS (user query rendered as HTML)
AUDIT: Identifies inline onclick as a JavaScript injection vector
expectations_missed: FIXES: Clears container safely using replaceChildren() or a removeChild loop — agent uses innerHTML = '' instead
additional_observations: Agent added a useful bonus fix for the race condition in addComment() (missing await on the POST)
The private #escapeHtml method is defined but only applied to the element ID attribute, where it is unnecessary since .id assignment does not parse HTML
